truth_boundary: Community terminology only. Static geometry may nominate a role; causal runtime evidence must confirm it; live ExtremeCraft evidence is a separate final gate.

definition: Principal TNT cohort supplying launch velocity.
static: large watered bank feeding a shared chamber
runtime: source-attributed explosion followed by measured payload velocity change

definition: Accumulates and releases a dense falling-block cohort.
static: sand shaft with pistons, gates, webs, or observers
runtime: falling-block count, release tick, density, and order at barrel entry

definition: TNT cohort that drives sand or another payload into its stack or impact path.
static: bank beside a payload chamber
runtime: attributed explosion and payload velocity delta

definition: TNT plus falling-block package intended to damage a water-protected cell.
static: sand and TNT share a guider or barrel
runtime: trajectory convergence, water contact, falling-payload overlap, and target damage

definition: Same-tick creation or processing order that changes which entity is moved or stacked first.
static: parallel same-tick lines merge into one chamber
runtime: stable attributed same-tick spawn order and outcome dependent on it

definition: Timed TNT package that clears slabs while preserving the primary stack.
static: toggled bank coupled to hammer or splitter
runtime: slab-height trajectory, slab destruction, and correct remaining stack

definition: Ordered explosions forming a repeated horizontal or vertical breach path.
static: multiple delayed cohorts on one route
runtime: ordered explosion centers and contiguous target destruction

definition: Extra sand and a dedicated swing or hammer used to exceed normal stack height.
static: overstack sand feed, stopper, and extra hammer
runtime: separate sand cohort, restacking motion, and above-baseline stack height

definition: Coordinated opening, one-shot sand placement, and follow-up payload intended to beat regeneration.
static: OSRB sand feed, OS hammer or restack path, companion toggles
runtime: regen destruction, correct sand position, and follow-up breach before restore

definition: Game-ticked TNT sequence creating an extended vertical explosion path.
static: several staged TNT banks and a vertical chamber
runtime: consecutive intended cohorts and ordered vertical explosion centers

definition: Split or paired TNT package intended to keep a destructive path through cobweb-style defenses.
static: paired same-tick banks with splitter or swing
runtime: cohorts preserve separation and create a measured midair web-course breach

definition: Nuke-like path produced by moving TNT through a sand stack using timing and entity order.
static: small nuke source tightly coupled to sand and hammer
runtime: TNT enters stack at intended height and creates repeatable vertical path

definition: Delayed secondary package intended to punish patching after primary impact.
static: separate tower, late timing line, dedicated toggle
runtime: delayed attributed cohort destroys a reproduced patch course

definition: Second controlled impact or hybrid package in one cannon cycle.
static: quick-pulse toggle and second hybrid delay
runtime: two distinct cohorts, measured delay, and added target change

definition: Impulse stage that separates or offsets payload cohorts before final motion.
static: small asymmetric TNT source beside a shared chamber
runtime: measured velocity split and predicted failure when removed

definition: Asymmetric alignment and impulse system producing a lateral shot.
static: lateral chambers, alignment pistons, splitter, alternate guiders
runtime: stable lateral velocity and impact point with attributed splitter and side impulse

definition: Payload is driven to a backboard and redirected into a reverse stack or hybrid.
static: reverse TNT near hammer or slab-bust and alternate guider
runtime: trajectory sign change at intended point and final reverse stack

definition: Staged payload advancing along a surface before a later transition.
static: repeated surface-aligned propulsion stages
runtime: ordered route positions and correct terminal transition

definition: Payload is aligned, redirected, stacked, or exploded at an airborne waypoint.
static: separated motion chambers or L-shaped guider
runtime: measured airborne waypoint and attributed second impulse

definition: Community architecture using staged impulses for unusual vertical motion or long-range high-count stacking.
static: separated motion stages and asymmetric guiders
runtime: vertical velocity profile, impact count, and stage attribution

definition: Architecture producing required payload motion without a conventional dedicated hammer cohort.
static: shared power, splitter sand, no obvious isolated hammer
runtime: required impulse with no separate hammer-attributed explosion and repeat-fire integrity

definition: Builder-dependent label for additional staged power or directional force packages.
static: separate directional bank or alternate propulsion route
runtime: distinct cohort and separately measured velocity change

definition: Claimed approximately 384-class falling-block stack capability, not a dispenser count.
static: large-capacity sand path; filename is weak metadata
runtime: measured falling-block count and final stack result across repeated shots

definition: Claimed approximately 255-class falling-block stack capability.
static: high-capacity sand path; labels are weak metadata
runtime: measured falling-block count, stack result, and repeatability

definition: Auxiliary package intended to route through or destroy a named obstruction.
static: toggled auxiliary lines or alternate guider
runtime: specific obstruction destroyed while continuation remains intact

definition: Stage that arrests or positions a moving payload for the next phase.
static: small source opposite motion at a transition point
runtime: velocity reduction or sign change and repeatable stop coordinate

definition: Unresolved builder or filename label; precise mechanism remains unknown.
static: filename, sign, or builder label only
runtime: working labeled reference, builder explanation, and distinguishing causal trace

definition: A cannon is a three-dimensional sequence of TNT and falling-block cohorts where one explosion changes the velocity, alignment, or position of the next payload stage.
static: separate chambers and banks arranged around a shared payload route rather than one flat redstone plane
runtime: source-attributed explosion followed by a measurable velocity delta in the intended recipient cohort, repeated stage by stage
failures: flat wiring with no physical chambers
payload receives impulse from wrong side
